summary: A dense friendship relation among users sharing three songs has quadratic input and output size, and the friendship-driven shared-listening join matches that worst-case bound.

optimality.argument: Let U users all listen to the same three songs on one day and let Friendship contain every unordered user pair. Then L = 3U, F = U(U-1)/2 = Theta(L^2), and all F rows must be returned. The query scans the friendship relation and processes at most O(L^2) logical pairs of listening facts before grouping and deduplication.

replacement_checks.evidence: Three universally shared songs and a complete friendship relation force Theta(U^2) output rows from L = 3U distinct listening facts and F = Theta(U^2) friendship rows.
The shared-listening join has at most one logical combination per pair of distinct listening facts after deduplication, while friendship access contributes linear work in F.
Authored fixtures cover exactly two versus three songs, duplicate logs, songs split across dates, nonfriends, repeated qualification across dates, independent qualifying pairs, and empty activity.
